findings.description: Caller-owned opaque document IDs could be mistaken for completed requirements.
findings.repair: completed_requirements remains empty until a future tenant-scoped case/document binding validates evidence.

findings.description: Cited requirement existence could be promoted to case satisfaction.
findings.repair: supported document requirements are downgraded to inferred_for_review and steps remain blocked.

findings.description: Assessment replay needed output-specific validation and corruption handling.
findings.repair: assessment validator selected for replay; corrupt bytes invalidated without marker leakage.
evidence: exact replay HTTP and PostgreSQL smoke

findings.description: Narrative facts and constraints would have been persisted in response replay.
findings.repair: response schema and mapper require empty narrative arrays and preserve only opaque references.

findings.description: A valid assessment might conceal an invalid intermediate workflow.
findings.repair: handler validates canonical ProcedureWorkflow before mapping/validating assessment.

open_global_findings: OS Electoral consumer interoperability is unproved.
Corpus, retrieval quality, cases, frontend/accessibility and platform operations remain incomplete.
